name: Elbmesh Implement Runtime Slice
title: Implement a Minimal Elbmesh Runtime Slice
summary: Make existing failing Elbmesh tests pass with the smallest production change while preserving runtime and architecture boundaries.
description: Use this task after failing tests or an explicit test plan exists for an Elbmesh runtime, manifest, reaction, view, journal, or trace slice.

failingTestEvidence.description: Failing command/output summary or test-plan reference.

validations.title: Confirm failing tests or test plan
criteria: Failing tests or an explicit test plan exist before production implementation starts.
If tests appear wrong, implementation stops and the mismatch is reported instead of silently rewriting tests.

validations.title: Review architecture boundary preservation
passCriteria: Implementation is slice-focused and does not include unplanned refactors.
Resource replay remains deterministic and uses Resource Events only.
External calls, Reaction execution, Views, Queries, journals, and execution traces stay on their documented side of the boundary.
Verification commands and any infeasible checks are recorded.

blocks.markdown: Implement `{{ task.input.sliceName }}` using the existing failing-test evidence: {{ task.input.failingTestEvidence }}.

items: Inspect the failing tests, source, docs, PPP task bundle, and task card before editing.
Keep the implementation inside the active phase and task card, making the smallest production change that satisfies the test contract.
Preserve explicit traits and Elbmesh vocabulary; do not hide domain behavior behind macros.
Keep replay/apply deterministic, with external calls only through declared External Operations and Resource event streams containing only Resource Events.
Stop and report the mismatch to the planning workflow if tests appear wrong; do not silently rewrite tests to match implementation preferences.
Run fmt, clippy, and tests when feasible; record blockers and architecture impact.

criteria.text: The targeted failing tests now pass without weakening their intent.
Rust quality gates pass or each infeasible command is explained.
